plans.title: Accelerated SWE Plan
why: This ordering pushes the core CS courses earlier so students spend the first half building strong technical fundamentals and the second half applying them to projects, internships, and market-facing work.
Software Engineering and Web Development are intentionally moved earlier because they shape practical engineering mindset and help students structure personal work sooner.
Electives are shifted later to preserve flexibility and avoid missing topics that may only be offered in specific terms.
General requirements are pushed back where possible to free higher-focus semesters for deeper technical and practical growth.
Challenging sequencing is viewed as a positive signal of discipline and follow-through, which compounds beyond university.
The plan is career-oriented: optimize early decisions around employability and iterate as new opportunities appear.
plans.closing: I think I'm decently pragmatic in thinking that, you are here, doing this bachelors degree, to make money in some way shape or form. So why not make that your goal from the very beginning? And actively plan for it and update your plan with new input as you learn more.
